raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the response focus primarily on technical correctness and claims, or on writing clarity and presentation?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites judgment about the framing of the contribution and whether the stated thesis coheres at the paper-draft level.","R1.2":"The artifact contains apparent tensions between claimed non-smooth acceleration and a theorem assuming smoothness with a sublinear rate, making logical consistency load-bearing.","R1.3":"The empirical and theoretical claims need to be checked against the reported theorem and CIFAR-10 result as presented.","R1.4":"The artifact makes explicit first-procedure and improvement-over-prior-work claims, so novelty assessment is centrally implicated.","R1.6":"A general reaction to a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection of a vague opinion request must disclose uncertainty about the intended type of feedback.","RX.2":"The response would need to avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.","RX.3":"The broad delegation still requires staying within evaluation of the provided research-writing artifact.","RX.4":"Potentially harmful downstream effects include endorsing unsupported technical or novelty claims without qualification.","RX.5":"Judgments should remain traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus primarily on technical correctness and claims, or on writing clarity and presentation?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites judgment about the framing of the contribution and whether the stated thesis coheres at the paper-draft level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains apparent tensions between claimed non-smooth acceleration and a theorem assuming smoothness with a sublinear rate, making logical consistency load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical and theoretical claims need to be checked against the reported theorem and CIFAR-10 result as presented.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes explicit first-procedure and improvement-over-prior-work claims, so novelty assessment is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: A general reaction to a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague opinion request must disclose uncertainty about the intended type of feedback.
rationale.RX.2: The response would need to avoid overstating what can be concluded from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.3: The broad delegation still requires staying within evaluation of the provided research-writing artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Potentially harmful downstream effects include endorsing unsupported technical or novelty claims without qualification.
rationale.RX.5: Judgments should remain traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact.
